recent_calls.summary: The user called and requested to speak with Daniel. The agent attempted to transfer the call to the requested person and successfully completed the transfer.
The user called and requested to speak with Dan Lagarse. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to the appropriate live agent successfully.

The user called to provide details for a home warranty service request regarding an air conditioning unit that is not cooling. The agent confirmed the dispatch number, service address, and contact information, and informed the user that someone from Budget Heating, Cooling & Plumbing would reach out shortly. The user declined information about maintenance programs and ended the call politely.

Debra called to schedule a repair service for her air conditioner under her American Home Shield home warranty. After some difficulty locating her dispatch number, she provided it, and the agent confirmed the details and assured that someone would contact her shortly. Debra declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests.

The call was initiated by the user but the conversation was largely unintelligible and disjointed, with the agent repeatedly trying to assist. The user expressed some confusion and frustration, and the call ended abruptly without resolution.

The user accepted the quotation for an 88-day service and requested an appointment. The agent asked if the service was related to a home warranty to direct the call appropriately. The call was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to schedule a cooling system service under their American Home Shield home warranty. The agent collected all necessary information, including the dispatch number, service address, and issue details, and confirmed the user's request for same-day service if possible. The user provided positive feedback about the interaction with the AI assistant.
The user called customer service and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 12 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user called customer service and was promptly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The call lasted 14 seconds and ended with a successful transfer.
The user requested to speak with a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak to a representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak to customer service, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called to inquire about coverage for repairs under their home warranty. The agent clarified the service area and explained the recall window for covered repairs. The user had questions but did not request any services or follow-up.

The user, Marvin Harris, called to check the status of an appointment. The agent was unable to find the appointment in the system and offered to transfer the user to a live representative, which the user accepted. The call was then successfully transferred to a live agent.

The user requested to speak to a live customer service representative, and the agent promptly transferred the call to the appropriate live agent. The transfer was completed successfully within a short call duration.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called to schedule an air conditioning installation and initially requested a Monday 6 PM slot. The agent offered alternative available times, and the user selected June 1st at 4 PM. Although the booking attempt failed due to a scheduling conflict, the agent assured the user that a human agent would follow up to confirm the appointment.

The user called to speak with Daniel or someone in ownership but the agent did not have direct contact information. The agent attempted to transfer the call to a live agent who could assist further, and the transfer was successful.

The user called to inquire about a maintenance visit expected between 8 and 10 AM but the technician had not arrived yet. The agent attempted to assist by transferring the call to a live agent for further help. The call ended with a successful transfer.

The user called to inquire about the arrival time of their technician appointment but the agent was unable to find the appointment in the system. The user requested to be transferred to a live representative for further assistance, and the call was successfully transferred.
The call was very brief, with the agent greeting the user and asking how to assist, but the user hung up immediately without responding.

The user called to reschedule a standard heating and cooling service and identified themselves as the owner. The agent gathered necessary details and then transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to inquire about plumbing services and provided their ZIP code. The agent attempted to assist, but the user repeatedly asked to speak to a person and expressed frustration. The call ended abruptly with the user hanging up.
The user called and requested to speak to Robert James or a representative. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Isaac from American Home Shield called to follow up on an AC work order for Cassandra Eason at 24 Biltmore Fair, Bolivar Boliville, Illinois. The agent confirmed the appointment details and informed Isaac that the technician Vernon was scheduled for May 28. Isaac requested a diagnosis update, which the agent promised to relay to the team for follow-up.

The user, Camren Scaife, called to schedule an installation appointment for replacing their cooling system. The agent confirmed details, clarified that the service was not covered under warranty, and booked the appointment for Monday, June 1st at 4 PM. The user declined to provide feedback at the end of the call.

The user called to schedule an appointment related to cooling services. The agent asked clarifying questions about the nature of the service and whether it was related to a home warranty plan, but the call ended abruptly before completion.

The user inquired about ductwork rerouting as part of a home remodel and provided detailed project information. The agent collected the user's contact details, confirmed the service address, and booked an appointment for the same day at 4 PM. The user was satisfied with the interaction and gave positive feedback.

The user called to inquire about the status of a service request for Dorothy Sparrell related to air conditioning at 5005 Forest Boulevard, East Saint Louis. The agent confirmed the details but could not find an appointment and offered to transfer the user to a live representative, which the user accepted. The call ended with a successful transfer to a live agent.
The user requested to speak with a live agent, and the initial agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.
The user requested to speak with a real person, and the agent promptly transferred the call to a live agent. The transfer was successful, and the call ended shortly after.

The user called Budget and was immediately transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

The user called to schedule a heating and cooling service appointment for their air conditioning not working at their home. The agent collected all necessary details, including the American Home Shield dispatch number, and confirmed the appointment request for today. The user expressed interest in receiving information about home maintenance programs, and the call ended with the agent confirming follow-up and the user declining to provide feedback.
The agent greeted the user and attempted to connect them with a live representative. The call was then successfully transferred to another agent for further assistance.

Kevin Cash called to check the arrival time of the technician for his appointment. The agent confirmed the appointment details and noted that the air conditioner is not working to inform the technician. The call concluded with no further requests from Kevin.

The user called Budget to inquire about air conditioner service in Illinois, providing their ZIP code 62204. The agent confirmed the service location and the type of service requested. The call ended shortly after without further details or service arrangements.

Isaac from American Home Shield called to follow up on an AC work order and confirmed details including the service address, issue, and preferred service time. The agent confirmed the information and noted Isaac's interest in receiving more information about home maintenance programs. The call ended positively with Isaac providing positive feedback and no further questions.
The user called to inquire about air conditioner service in Illinois and provided their ZIP code. The call ended abruptly after the user repeated 'Hello' without further interaction.

Leslie from American Home Shield called to inquire about the earliest scheduling for a work order related to an air conditioning issue. The virtual assistant gathered details about the service request and confirmed the urgency as non-emergency. Leslie was then transferred to a live agent for further assistance.

The user called to check if Budget received a notification from their home warranty and to get a status update on their service request. The agent collected the user's service address, phone number, and name, then confirmed the details and informed the user that the team would follow up with a status update. The user requested to speak to a live person, and the call was successfully transferred to a live agent.
No conversation happened.

The user called to request air conditioning service as their unit is blowing air but not cooling. The agent gathered necessary details and informed the user of the earliest available technician slots, which were all after the requested time. The user declined the offered times and ended the call politely.

The user called to follow up on a service order and was directed by American Home Shield to schedule an appointment. The agent acknowledged the issue but transferred the call to the billing team for further assistance with payment and repair scheduling.

The user called to confirm the technician appointment scheduled for May 29th between 2 and 3 PM. The agent verified the user's name and phone number, corrected the phone number, and confirmed the appointment details. The user did not request any changes or additional assistance.

The user called to schedule an appointment for a central air unit service under their American Home Shield warranty and wanted to know the scheduled time for the service. The agent gathered the necessary information and transferred the call to a live agent for further assistance.

Julie called to schedule an HVAC cooling system service under her American Home Shield home warranty. The agent collected all necessary details including the dispatch number, service issue, preferred appointment time, and confirmed the information with Julie. Julie declined information about maintenance programs and feedback requests, and the agent assured a follow-up for appointment confirmation.
The user called and was quickly transferred to a live agent for further assistance. The initial agent, Sophie, did not engage in a detailed conversation but facilitated the transfer promptly.

The user reported that their air conditioning system was not working and requested immediate service. The agent scheduled a provisional appointment for the same day at 2 PM and confirmed the user's contact details and service address. The call ended with the user declining to provide feedback.
The user called to speak with someone about their air conditioner. The agent acknowledged the request and transferred the call to a live agent who could assist further.

The user, a vendor for Budget Heating, Cooling, and Plumbing, called to speak with CG. The agent confirmed the user's ZIP code and asked how to assist, but the user requested to speak to a specific person and then ended the call.
